description_long: This object is a large ground-mounted solar panel array, consisting of multiple rectangular photovoltaic modules arranged in a grid pattern. The panels are supported by a robust metallic frame structure with vertical posts anchoring the system to the ground. The solar modules have a dark blue to black surface, typical of monocrystalline or polycrystalline silicon cells, and are bordered by metallic frames. The supporting structure appears to be made of metal, likely aluminum or galvanized steel, providing both stability and corrosion resistance. The array is elevated above the ground, allowing for airflow beneath the panels and easy maintenance access. No visible electrical inverters or cabling are present in the images, and the array is designed for stationary, high-capacity solar energy generation in open urban or suburban environments.
description: A large, ground-mounted solar panel array with multiple dark blue modules supported by a metallic frame and posts, designed for stationary solar energy generation.
description_view_0: View 0 shows the solar array from a slightly elevated angle, highlighting the depth and multiple rows of panels supported by metallic posts.
description_view_1: View 1 presents the array from a side angle, emphasizing the tilt of the panels and the linear arrangement of the supporting structure.
description_view_2: View 2 offers a direct front view, displaying the full grid of solar panels and the uniformity of the array.
description_view_3: View 3 mirrors view 1 from the opposite side, again showing the panel tilt and the structural supports beneath.
attribute_panel_orientation: tilted at approximately 25 degrees, orientation appears fixed
attribute_mounting_structure_type: ground-mounted with metallic posts and aluminum frame
attribute_array_configuration: multiple rows in a rectangular grid, approximately 6x8 panels
